methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $250M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 12 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $18.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Quantum-Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $8B

data.narrative:
Kodiak AI is a Series C defense and security company that has raised $250M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 12 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies comprising 2 public and 10 private firms, including notable comparables such as Helsing ($18.0B, Series D), Saronic ($9.2B, Series D), Quantum-Systems ($8.0B, Series C), Chaos Industries ($4.5B, Series D), and Dream Security ($3.0B, Series B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling, anchoring to the $250M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yields $425M. Peer-median analysis across the 12-company comp set—which ranges from $300M to $18.0B—produces a median valuation of $1.8B, reflecting the wide dispersion characteristic of early-stage defense tech.

The triangulated valuation range is $361M (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median estimate of $1.1B. The wide range reflects two material tensions: stage-progression suggests a more conservative $425M valuation, while peer-median placement anchors to $1.8B, driven substantially by outlier high performers like Helsing and Quantum-Systems at Series C/D. Key uncertainties include Kodiak's relative technology maturity, customer traction specificity, and regulatory pathway clarity versus the comp set. The $1.1B median represents appropriate mid-point positioning pending resolution of these factors.
